title: Crispy Korean Hake Namul
description: A crispy and flavorful Korean dish made with hake, perfect for a post-workout meal. It features a mustardy sauce and a crunchy texture.
instructions: Rinse the hake fillets under cold water, then pat them dry with paper towels.
In a blender or food processor, combine soy sauce, Gochujang, brown sugar, garlic, and ginger, and blend until smooth.
Pour the sauce into a large bowl, then add the hake fillets and marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Remove the hake fillets from the marinade, allowing any excess to drip off.
Add the hake fillets to the skillet and cook for 3-4 minutes on each side, until crispy and golden brown.
Transfer the hake fillets to a plate, then reduce the heat to medium and add the sesame oil to the skillet.
Add the chopped green onions to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Serve the hake fillets with the green onion sauce spooned over the top.
Pressure cook the leftover sauce for 5 minutes to create a thick and syrupy namul sauce.
Garnish with additional chopped green onions, if desired.
